display_text: Does your AI system or use case fall within any of the exclusion categories listed in Article 2 of the EU AI Act? Select the option that best applies.
hint_text: Article 2 divides exclusions into 'hard' (assessment ends, no obligations) and 'soft' (partial, assessment continues to R3). Hard exclusions: military/national security, and third-country law enforcement. Soft exclusions: scientific research, open-source components under free licence, and purely personal non-professional use. For soft exclusions the system may still fall under Article 5 prohibited-practice rules, so the assessment must continue. Note: the research exclusion applies only when scientific R&D is the sole purpose of the system. Systems already placed on the market or put into service do not qualify. Open-source exclusion applies until the component is integrated into a high-risk, prohibited, GPAI, or transparency-obligated system. Source: Article 2(6), 2(8), 2(10), 2(12).

options.label: The AI system is used exclusively for military, national security, defence purposes, OR by a public authority in a third country for law enforcement and judicial cooperation with the EU
AI research and development — my system exists solely for scientific R&D
Open source — my AI component is provided under a free and open source licence
Personal use only — I use this AI system for purely personal, non-professional activity
None of the above — no exclusion applies; the AI Act applies in full to this system
